commands.review_summary:
The PR changes macOS Nix-mode defaults lookup to skip `UserDefaults(suiteName:)` when the stable suite name matches the running bundle identifier, and adds tests for same-bundle and different-bundle behavior.

PR surface: Other +16. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main uses `ai.openclaw.mac` as both the bundle identifier and stable defaults suite name, then calls `UserDefaults(suiteName: launchdLabel)` in the launch-time Nix-mode path. I did not live-run the macOS app in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is real macOS launch proof or maintainer verification, not a concrete code repair that ClawSweeper can safely automate.

target.title: fix(macos): skip redundant defaults suite
target.body:
## Summary
- Avoid opening a redundant `UserDefaults(suiteName:)` domain when the running macOS app bundle identifier already matches OpenClaw's stable launchd/defaults label.
- Keep the existing Nix-mode fallback for app builds whose bundle identifier differs, where the stable suite is still needed.
- Add focused coverage for both the redundant-suite skip and the different-bundle fallback path.

## Linked context
Closes #88909

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: macOS logs `Using your own bundle identifier as an NSUserDefaults suite name does not make sense` when the app opens a defaults suite named `ai.openclaw.mac` while running as bundle `ai.openclaw.mac`.
- Real environment tested: source-level verification in this contributor environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused static assertion over `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ProcessInfo+OpenClaw.swift` and `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/NixModeStableSuiteTests.swift`; `git diff --check -- apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ProcessInfo+OpenClaw.swift apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/NixModeStableSuiteTests.swift`.
- Evidence after fix: the assertion confirmed `stableNixDefaults(bundleIdentifier:)` returns `nil` when the bundle identifier equals the suite name, `isNixMode` uses that helper, the direct `UserDefaults(suiteName: launchdLabel)` call is gone from the runtime source, and the new tests cover same-bundle and different-bundle behavior.
- Observed result after fix: the app path no longer constructs the nonsensical same-name suite while preserving the stable suite for differing bundle identifiers.
- What was not tested: I could not run `swift test --filter NixModeStableSuiteTests` because this Linux contributor runner has no `swift` binary installed, and I could not launch the macOS app here.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no macOS runtime proof is available from this environment; this is a focused source/test fix for the exact warning path reported.

## Tests and validation
- Passed: focused Python static assertion checking the runtime helper, removed direct same-name suite call, and added same/different bundle tests.
- Passed: `git diff --check -- apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ProcessInfo+OpenClaw.swift apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/NixModeStableSuiteTests.swift`.
- Not run: `swift test --filter NixModeStableSuiteTests` because `swift` is not installed on this runner.

## Risk checklist
- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, macOS warning noise should stop for the default app bundle.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No config format change; same-bundle app builds now rely on `UserDefaults.standard`, which is the same defaults domain the redundant suite name would map to.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: preserving Nix-mode detection for alternate bundle identifiers; mitigated by keeping the stable-suite fallback and adding focused coverage.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review. Remaining proof item is a macOS runtime launch check to confirm the stderr warning is gone.
target.author: charles-openclaw
